subject: DevOps
title: Immutable Infrastructure
description: A micro-lesson that makes Immutable Infrastructure usable.

hook.text: The server was SSH'd into 47 times for patches. Nobody knew its state. Snowflake servers are bombs.

buildup.text: Mutable infrastructure changes in place. Over time, servers drift from their intended state.

discovery.text: Immutable infrastructure never changes. Need an update? Build a new image, deploy, destroy the old.

twist.text: Every deploy is a fresh, known state. No drift. Rollback means redeploying the prior image.

climax.text: Packer builds machine images. Docker builds container images. Both enable immutable patterns.

punchline.text: Don't fix servers. Replace them. Immutability kills drift.

quiz.question: How do you update an immutable server?
options: Build a new image, deploy it, and destroy the old one
SSH in and apply patches manually
Edit the configuration files on the running server
